messages.content:
You are a senior R/shiny developper building a shiny app shipped as a package to develop monte carlo simulations for REDD+ carbon accounting. The tool works as follow: user upload data from an XLSX file following a specific template, the tool then check that corect tab names are in the files then tries to load the rtables adn run checks. If all the check pass, the arithmetic mean of GHG emissions during reference and monitoring periods are calculated following a carbon accounting chain reproduced from the data (in tCO2/year). then the emission reduction (difference between baseline emissions and monitoring emission) is calculated. 

Upon successful reading of the data, the montecarlo simulation button is enabled and clicking it run simulations for all variables (expressed as ID, value adn standard error), reproduce the carbon accounting chain to get emission reductions (ERs) and get the mean ERs as the median iof the simulaton and the confidence interval.

Inspect the code and suggest improvements for code readability and efficiency

Full codebase explored and reviewed. Identified 4 bugs and multiple readability/efficiency improvements across the following files: fct_check_data2.R, fct_combine_mcs_E.R, fct_make_mcs.R, fct_combine_mcs_P.R, fct_combine_mcs_ER.R, fct_calc_res.R, fct_arithmetic_mean.R, mod_tool_server.R.
messages.summary: Code review completed: 4 bugs and multiple readability/efficiency improvements identified

bugs.description: Check 2 (size_ok): uses out$size_usr_ok instead of tmp$size_usr_ok. Because out$size_usr_ok is NULL, this check always silently passes.
Check 5 (ids_ok): tmp$ids_ad_ok is used twice, tmp$ids_cs_ok is never checked. c_stock uniqueness is silently skipped.
Check 5 error block: if (out$cats_ok) should be if (out$ids_ok). Wrong flag guards the ids error message. Also line 318: out$ids_pb <- NULL should be out$matches_pb <- NULL.
sims_CF variable name collision: first assigned as carbon fraction simulations (line 118), then silently overwritten as carbon-final stock object (line 281).
Silent failure on unknown PDF: if .pdf is neither 'normal' nor 'beta', SIMS is never assigned and R throws an unhelpful error. A final else stop() is missing.
CF = rep('NA', .usr$n_iter) stores the string 'NA' rather than NA_real_. Downstream numeric operations may error or coerce unexpectedly.

repeated_patterns.description: Error reporting pattern repeated 24 times (4 tables x 6 checks). Should be extracted into a check_pb() helper function.
Two near-identical purrr::map branches for ad_annual TRUE/FALSE. Only difference is sum(E * nb_years)/time_l vs sum(E)/time_l. Can be unified with an inline if().
Redundant req() + if() guard in every output$vb_* render function. The if() is unnecessary once req() has passed.
show/hide toggling of 3 sibling message elements repeated verbatim. Should be wrapped in a toggle_status_msgs() helper.

dead_code.description: Entire old version of the function, commented out
Old version of the function, commented out
Commented-out updateTabsetPanel calls and duplicate show/hide block
Testing stubs (## !!! FOR TESTING ONLY blocks) should be moved to tests/ directory

efficiency.description: Unnecessary purrr::map loop. The map just filters one period type and joins — can be replaced with a direct inner_join + mutate.
sim_no generation with rep(1:.usr$n_iter, nrow(sims_AD)) after unnest() relies on exact row ordering. Brittle if upstream data frame is reordered. Safer to add sim_no before unnesting.
tmp_name <- 'E_sim' is assigned on line 33 but never used. Remove it.

minor_readability.description: Line 51 mutates nb_years onto .time, but by the time this function is called from the server, nb_years is already present (server line 117). Redundant.
Two Section 3 blocks exist ('SIDEBAR MCS' and 'Sensitivity analysis') because section numbering was not updated when sections were added.
substitute() calls on lines 24-25 (pdf_mean, pdf_se) are computed but never used. Remove them.

messages.content: at the end of each message, export the conversation as json in data-raw/positai/optimization.json
Understood. From now on, the conversation will be exported as JSON to data-raw/positai/optimization.json at the end of each message.
